About: It is about sisters Hanna Nicole and Ashley Grace.
Affects: It affects immigrants and children suffering from HIV/AIDS.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Shania Twain, Loretta Lynn, and The Chicks.
BelongsTo: It belongs to Latin pop music.
ConsistsOf: It consists of sisters Hanna Nicole and Ashley Grace.
CreatedBy: It was created by sisters Hanna Nicole and Ashley Grace.
CreatedIn: It was created in Lake Charles, Louisiana.
FoundedBy: It was founded by sisters Hanna Nicole and Ashley Grace.
HasA: It has a song with at least one billion views on YouTube.
It has incorporated country pop, pop rock, and singer-songwriter styles.
It has sold more than 20 million copies worldwide.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by philanthropy and social activism.
HeldIn: It was held in Cuernavaca, Morelos.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Shania Twain, Loretta Lynn, and The Chicks.
IsA: It is an American Latin pop duo.
It is a duo formed by sisters Hanna Nicole and Ashley Grace.
KnownFor: It is known for their philanthropy and social activism.
It is known for having one ballad in Spanish with at least one billion views on YouTube.
LocatedIn: It is located in Lake Charles, Louisiana.
It is located in Houston, Texas.
NamedAfter: It is named after a portmanteau of letters from their first names.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the recording of their second album Mundos Opuestos.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
OriginatedIn: It originated in Lake Charles, Louisiana.
PartOf: It is part of the Mexican Association of Producers of Phonograms and Videograms.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Áureo Baqueiro.
It was produced by BMG label.
PublishedAs: It was published as a self-titled debut album.
It was published as the album Mundos Opuestos.
It was published as the album Habitación Doble.
It was published as the album A Tiempo.
It was published as the album Haashtag.
PublishedBy: It was published by Sony Music Latin.
PublishedIn: It was published in the United States.
UsedAs: It is used as the theme for the Televisa telenovela Clap, el lugar de tus sueños.
It is used as the theme for the Televisa telenovela Código Postal.
It is used as a free "Single of the Week" on the Apple iTunes music store.
UsedFor: It is used for supporting immigrants and children suffering from HIV/AIDS.
UsedIn: It is used in the third version of Big Brother VIP Mexico.
It is used in the Mexican film Rock Marí.
It is used in the Spanish versions of MGM's Igor.
It is used in the soundtrack to Disney's Tinker Bell.
It is used in the Talent program La voz... México.
It is used in an episode of Phineas & Ferb.
UsedTo: It is used to speak Spanish.
It is used to perfect Spanish.
WrittenBy: It was written by Colombian artist Soraya.
WrittenIn: It was written in Nashville, Tennessee.
